# Design: MVE Timeline UX Polish (Scene-Sync, In-Scene Reorder, Compact Lanes)

<!-- generated by /feature-intake on 2026-07-12 — 3 follow-up items from mve-textblock-order-sync / mve-textblock-live-wpm-shrink -->

## Problem & Intent

Three known UX limitations remain from the MVE audio-dialog work (T32, Slice 1 Stufe B):

1. **Scene-Sync-Latenz**: `syncSceneForDraftLine` debounces 800ms before the pink Structure-Scene shell resizes to match typed text. Feels sluggish for fast typists.
2. **Kein In-Scene-Reorder**: `useMveTextBlockLaneDrop` / `moveTextBlockToScene` only support moving a text block into a *different* scene. Dropping within the same scene is a no-op (`move-text-block-to-scene.ts` returns early when `line.sceneId === targetSceneId`).
3. **Leere Dialog-Lanes zu hoch**: `LANE_UI.heightDialogCompact` (280px) is applied to every character dialog lane regardless of content, wasting vertical space in the audio sidebar when a lane has no text blocks/clips yet.

## Non-Goals

- No ms-tick timeline refactor (`DEFAULT_FRAME_RATE`) — reuse existing sec-based spans.
- No full ripple-engine integration for the live scene-width preview — direct DOM style only (no sibling repositioning during typing).
- No cross-scene reorder changes (T32 cross-scene move stays as-is).
- No change to film `structureDriven` policy (audio-only, `contentDriven`).

## Options Considered

### Slice 1 — Scene-Sync UX

- **Option A (debounce only):** Drop `SCENE_SYNC_DEBOUNCE_MS` 800→300ms. Simple, but the *real* scene shell still only updates after a backend round-trip.
- **Option B (DOM preview only):** Mirror the pink Structure scene shell width live via direct DOM style mutation (same pattern as `applyStructureDragFollow` in `ripple-engine/preview.ts`), keyed off `data-scene-id`, no React state/ripple. Backend sync stays debounced.
- **Option C (both, recommended):** 300ms debounce *and* an immediate DOM-only width preview computed with the existing pure `maxVisualContentEndSecInScene`. The preview is self-correcting (recomputed every keystroke from current draft) and gets authoritatively overwritten once the debounced `syncSceneForDraftLine` → `resizeSceneForContent` round-trip invalidates the timeline query.

**Decision: Option C.** Reuses existing pure layout math; no new state, no ripple broadcast to sibling scenes (matches "without ripple per keystroke").

### Slice 2 — In-Scene Reorder

- **Option A:** Full drag-preview with live insertion marker between siblings. Higher fidelity, bigger diff (DOM measurement + preview state).
- **Option B (recommended):** On drop, if `targetSceneId === draggedLine.sceneId`, compute target index from pointer x vs. sibling visual-span midpoints (reusing `resolveMveLineVisualSpanMap`), then persist sequential `orderIndex` (0..n-1) for the scene's siblings via `updateMveLine`. No live insertion marker in this slice (existing `dragOverSceneId` highlight stays as move-target feedback).

**Decision: Option B.** Matches existing `dragOverSceneId` and `moveTextBlockToScene` command-on-drop pattern; visual stacking refresh happens automatically via existing `resolveMveLineVisualSpanMap` (order-index driven) after query invalidation.

### Slice 3 — Compact Dialog Lane Height

- **Option A:** Shrink `heightDialogCompact` globally. Rejected — breaks lanes that already have text blocks (toolbar + textarea need ~280px).
- **Option B (recommended):** New `LANE_UI.heightDialogEmpty` tier (= `heightCompact`, 152px) used only when a dialog lane has **zero** MVE lines and **zero** clips. `resolveLaneHeightPx` gets an optional `hasContent` parameter (default `true` — backward compatible for existing callers/tests). Sidebar and content rows both receive the same computed height from `AudioClipLaneTracks`.

**Decision: Option B.**

## Runtime Matrix

| Axis | Slice 1 (Scene-Sync) | Slice 2 (Reorder) | Slice 3 (Lane Height) |
|------|----------------------|-------------------|------------------------|
| Local (desktop) | yes | yes | yes |
| Cloud session | n/a (local MVE only) | n/a | n/a |
| Appwrite Functions | skip (frozen) | skip (frozen) | skip (frozen) |

MVE persistence is local-only (`isLocalProfile()` gate in `useMveLines`); no cloud/Appwrite path touched.

## Cross-Domain Sign-Off

| Domain | Status |
|--------|--------|
| KISS | ✅ reuses existing pure functions, no new abstractions |
| SOLID | ✅ |
| DRY | ✅ `maxVisualContentEndSecInScene`, `resolveMveLineVisualSpanMap`, `sortLinesInScene` reused |
| Security | ✅ no new inputs beyond existing MVE line text/order |
| UI/UX | ✅ addresses all 3 named limitations |
| Scaling | ✅ DOM-only preview, no extra queries per keystroke |
| Testability | ✅ pure order/height functions unit-testable |
| Maintainability | ✅ small, localized diffs |

## Implementation Sketch

```
src/components/audio/AudioTimelineMveTextBlock.tsx   — 300ms debounce + live scene-width DOM preview
src/lib/ripple-engine/preview.ts                     — applyMveSceneWidthPreviewPx()
src/lib/mve/resolve-scene-at-timeline-sec.ts          — reorderLineOrderIndexes()
src/lib/mve/reorder-text-block-in-scene.ts            — new: persist in-scene reorder
src/lib/mve/move-text-block-to-scene.ts               — unchanged (same-scene no-op stays; caller routes to reorder instead)
src/hooks/useMveTextBlockLaneDrop.ts                  — detect same-scene drop → target index
src/hooks/useMveLines.ts                              — reorderLineInScene mutation
src/components/timeline/audio/AudioClipLaneContent.tsx — wire linesInLane + onReorderLineInScene
src/components/structure/timeline/tracks/StructureTimelineAudioLanes.tsx — handleReorderLineInScene
src/lib/audio-lane.ts                                 — heightDialogEmpty + hasContent param
src/components/timeline/audio/AudioClipLaneTracks.tsx — compute hasContent per lane
src/components/timeline/audio/AudioClipLaneSidebar.tsx — accept hasContent prop
```

## Ready for /implement

YES (user pre-approved; skip review gate before implementation, still run verify/audit gates).
